summary: @torbeck/heap ships an import-time remote-code dropper appended to a typosquatted heap library
details: The package impersonates @datastructures-js/heap: package.json copies the upstream description, repository (github.com/datastructures-js/heap), keywords, and author (Eyas Ranjous), but publishes under scope @torbeck at version 4.3.14. The legitimate Heap/MinHeap/MaxHeap source is copied verbatim into src/heap.js, and after `exports.Heap = Heap;` two ~45KB obfuscator.io-packed IIFEs are appended (string-array + rotating decoder + RC4-style char-code XOR + control-flow flattening + self-defending wrapper). At `require('@torbeck/heap')` time these IIFEs require child_process/fs/os/path/crypto/https/http, decrypt a destination URL from XORed 32-byte hex constants via an AES cipher, issue an https.request with 3xx redirect handling (up to 5 hops), stream the response into os.tmpdir(), chmod the file 0o755, and spawn it via child_process.spawn(process.execPath,[file],{detached:true,windowsHide:true}).unref() (or spawn(file,[]) detached+unref). A process.env marker plus respawn is used to gate/persist execution. None of child_process, https, or filesystem write+exec has any relation to a heap data-structure library.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
